meta.title: The Clever Monkey — Vocabulary Set 4
meta.subject: English Vocabulary
meta.description: All 16 words in context — sentence completion and word usage practice.

questions.question: I like _______ on my sandwich. It is yellow and tasty.
questions.hint: A yellow food made from milk
questions.explanation: 'Cheese' is a food made from milk. It is often yellow and is used in sandwiches, pizzas, and many other foods.

questions.question: We _______ the cookies. Each kid gets three.
questions.explanation: 'Divide' means to split into parts. When you divide the cookies equally, each person gets the same number.

questions.question: We have an _______ number of balls. We both have three.
questions.explanation: 'Equal' means the same. Both having three balls makes the amounts equal — no one has more.

questions.question: The game is _______. It is hard to win.
questions.hint: Hard and clever at the same time
questions.explanation: 'Tricky' means difficult in a clever way. A tricky game requires careful thought because it is not straightforward.

questions.question: I _______ my mom for making dinner.
questions.explanation: 'Thanked' means said thank you to someone. 'I thanked my mom' — I expressed my gratitude to her for making dinner.

questions.question: I walk _______ down the icy stairs.
questions.explanation: 'Carefully' means with great care and attention. Walking carefully on ice means moving slowly to avoid slipping.

questions.question: I _______ it will rain today, so I bring my umbrella.
questions.explanation: 'Believe' means to think something is true. 'I believe it will rain' — you think rain is likely based on what you see.

questions.question: I am _______ of watching scary movies. They frighten me!
questions.explanation: 'Afraid' means scared. Being 'afraid of scary movies' means you feel frightened by them.

questions.question: The boy _______ on the swing at the park. He went back and forth.
questions.explanation: 'Swung' is the past tense of 'swing.' The boy swung on the swing — he moved back and forth on it.

questions.question: The teacher _______ the prizes between the students so everyone got the same.
questions.explanation: 'Divided' means split into equal parts. The teacher divided the prizes so each student got the same number.

questions.question: The children looked _______ at the broken toy — they were very unhappy.
questions.explanation: 'Sadly' means in an unhappy way. The children looked sadly at the broken toy — their faces showed unhappiness.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about being fair:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'We play the game fairly.' 'Fairly' describes how we play — in a just and equal way, following the rules.

questions.question: Choose the sentence where 'fortune' is used CORRECTLY:
options: She fortune the cookies into two pieces.
He made a fortune mistake on the test.
Winning first place was a great fortune — what luck!
questions.explanation: 'Fortune' means great luck. 'A great fortune' means a wonderful stroke of luck — like winning first place unexpectedly.

questions.question: Arrange the words to make a sentence about the monkey's trick:
questions.explanation: Correct sentence: 'The pieces were not exactly the same.' This is what the monkey kept pretending — he said the pieces were unequal so he could eat more cheese.

questions.question: In the sentence 'The jungle was a mistake to enter,' 'mistake' is used as a noun.
questions.explanation: Yes! 'Mistake' is a noun — a thing. 'A mistake' is an error or wrong action. In this sentence, 'a mistake' is the subject's action of entering — it is treated as a thing that happened.
